protein_name: Mitochondrial GTPase involved in mitochondrial trafficking. Probably involved in control of anterograde transport of mitochondria and their subcellular distribution. Belongs to the mitochondrial Rho GTPase family. Ubiquitously expressed. Highly expressed in heart, liver, skeletal muscle, kidney and pancreas. 2 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.
